methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.0B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Observable Space is a Series A space & aerospace company founded by Dan Roelker and Rick Hedrick that has raised $20M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 public equities (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including ClearSpace, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, and Planet Labs at same or adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression anchors on the $20M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage step-ups, implying $40M. Peer-median methodology references four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (Cowboy Space Corporation at $2.0B Series B, Aalyria at $1.3B Series B, ClearSpace at $400M Series A, and Hubble Network at $400M Series B), yielding a median valuation of $850M with a range of $400M–$2.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; the public Space & Aerospace median P/S is 64.2x across the five tickers, but Observable Space's revenue is not on file.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a valuation range of $34M (low) to $977M (high), with a median of $445M. This wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in stage-appropriate relative valuation: the peer set spans two stages and shows significant scatter. Key uncertainties include Observable Space's revenue trajectory relative to peers, product maturity, and customer concentration relative to the Series B-weighted comp sample.
